briefdescription.para: Given an uninitialized instance of a red-black tree (rbtree)

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_init() is called with a pointer to a comparison function
Then tree is initialized, has zero nodes, null pointer to root and requested comparison function set

briefdescription.para: Given an uninitialized instance of a red-black tree node (rbnode) and some key

detaileddescription.para: When rbnode_init() is called with a pointer to that key
Then node is initialized, has null pointers to left and right children nodes, zeroed parent pointer with black color encoded and pointer to requested key set

briefdescription.para: Given an empty red-black tree

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_size() is called
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_size() is called
Then number of nodes in tree is returned

briefdescription.para: Given an empty red-black tree

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_empty() is called
Then 1 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_empty() is called
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_empty() is called
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given an empty red-black tree

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_insert() is called with a pointer to an initialized node
Then that node is a new root and sole node of the tree

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_insert() is called with a pointer to an initialized node with a key higher than root node's
Then that node is added to the tree and is root node's right child

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with two nodes, a root and its right child

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_insert() is called with a pointer to an initialized node with a key higher than that of existing nodes
Then that node is added to the tree, tree is rebalanced and newly added node is new root's right child

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_insert() is called with a pointer to an initialized node with a key higher than that of existing nodes
Then that node is added to the tree, tree is rebalanced and recolored

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_insert() is called with a pointer to an initialized node
Then that node is added to the tree, tree is rebalanced and recolored

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_insert() is called with a pointer to an initialized node
Then that node is added to the tree, tree is rebalanced and recolored

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbnode_prev() is called with a pointer to that node
Then null pointer is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with a root node and its left and right children

detaileddescription.para: When rbnode_prev() is called with a pointer to the root node
Then a pointer to the left child node is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a full red-black tree with 7 nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbnode_prev() is called with a pointer to the node with lowest key in the right sub-tree
Then a pointer to the root node is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbnode_next() is called with a pointer to that node
Then null pointer is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with a root node and its left and right children

detaileddescription.para: When rbnode_next() is called with a pointer to the root node
Then a pointer to the right child node is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a full red-black tree with 7 nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbnode_next() is called with a pointer to the node with highest key in the left sub-tree
Then a pointer to the root node is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_remove() is called with a pointer to that node
Then that node is removed from the tree, tree is empty

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with two nodes, a root and its right child

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_remove() is called with a pointer to the root node
Then that node is removed from the tree, remaining node is the new root

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with two nodes, a root and its right child

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_remove() is called with a pointer to the leaf node
Then that node is removed from the tree, tree's original root node is the only node in tree

briefdescription.para: Given a balanced red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_remove() is called multiple times with pointers to all nodes in tree
Then all nodes are removed from the tree, tree is empty

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with a root node and its left and right children

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_remove() is called with a pointer to the root node
Then root node is removed from the tree, original root's successor is the new root of the tree, tree has 2 nodes

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_remove() is called with a pointer to one of the nodes
Then that node is removed, tree is rebalanced and recolored

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_remove() is called with a pointer to one of the nodes
Then that node is removed, tree is rebalanced and recolored

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_remove() is called with a pointer to one of the nodes
Then that node is removed, tree is rebalanced and recolored

briefdescription.para: Given an empty red-black tree

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_contains() is called with a null pointer
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given an empty red-black tree

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_contains() is called with a pointer to a node not present in the tree
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_contains() is called with a pointer to that node in the tree
Then 1 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_contains() is called with a pointer to a node not present in the tree
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with two nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_contains() is called with a pointer to any node in the tree
Then 1 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with two nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_contains() is called with a pointer to a node not present in the tree
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given an empty red-black tree

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_find() is called with any key
Then null pointer is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_find() is called with the key of that node
Then pointer to the sole node is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with two nodes, a root and its left child

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_find() is called with the key of the left child node
Then pointer to the left child node is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with two nodes, a root and its right child

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_find() is called with the key of the right child node
Then pointer to the right child node is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_find() is called with a key value that's not stored in the tree
Then null pointer is returned

briefdescription.para: Given an empty red-black tree

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_root() is called
Then null pointer is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_root() is called
Then pointer to the sole node is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with 8 nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_root() is called
Then a pointer to the node that's at the root of the red-black tree is returned

briefdescription.para: Given an empty red-black tree

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_last() is called
Then null pointer is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_last() is called
Then pointer to the root node is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_last() is called
Then pointer to the node with greatest key is returned

briefdescription.para: Given an empty red-black tree

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_first() is called
Then null pointer is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with exactly one node

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_first() is called
Then pointer to the root node is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_first() is called
Then pointer to the node with lowest key is returned

briefdescription.para: Given N/A

detaileddescription.para: When rbnode_foreach() is used with a null pointer
Then no loop iterations are performed

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbnode_foreach() is used with a pointer to a node from the tree with non-extreme key value
Then body of the loop is executed for requested node and all its successors in order

briefdescription.para: Given an empty red-black tree

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_foreach() is used
Then no loop iterations are performed

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_foreach() is used
Then body of the loop is executed for all nodes from the tree in order of increasing node key values

briefdescription.para: Given a red-black tree with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When rbtree_foreach() is used and one of the nodes is removed
Then body of the loop is executed for all nodes from the tree and the removed node is not present in the tree

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen Library Unit Test Suite.

detaileddescription.para:
2020-2021 N7 Space Sp. z o.o.
Unit Test Suite was developed under a programme of, and funded by, the European Space Agency.

You may obtain a copy of the License at http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
